steps.title: Step 1
steps.content: Start from $36 = \frac{2K}{v^2}$. Multiply both sides by $v^2$ to clear the denominator: $36v^2 = 2K$.

steps.title: Step 2
steps.content: Divide both sides by 36 to isolate $v^2$: $v^2 = \frac{2K}{36}$.

steps.title: Step 3
steps.content: Since $v$ is positive, take the positive square root of both sides: $v = \sqrt{\frac{2K}{36}}$.

steps.title: Step 4
steps.content:
• The matching choice: $v=\sqrt{\frac{2K}{36}}$. Matches the derived expression exactly. A is correct.
  • $v=\sqrt{\frac{36}{2K}}$. This has the fraction inverted; it would follow from $v^2 = \frac{36}{2K}$, which is wrong. Eliminated.
  • $v=\frac{36^{2}}{2K}$. This is not a square root and does not follow from solving for $v$. Eliminated.
  • $v=\frac{2K}{36^{2}}$. This is also not a square root and incorrectly squares the denominator. Eliminated.
